title: ATP Execution Contract
description: Defines authority, policy, approval, credential access, and evidence requirements for governed agent execution under the Agent Trust Protocol.

version.description: Semantic version of the contract format.

authority.description: Organization-scoped authority URI. Format: org.{domain}.{permission}. Validated against the organization's capability registry.

template.description: ATP template ID. References a reusable policy template registered on DUAL.

actions.description: Permitted action names. The agent may only invoke actions listed here.

scope.description: Action-specific constraints. Schema varies by action type. These are enforceable policy constraints evaluated before execution, not prompt guidelines.

approval.description: Approval configuration. Defines whether human authorization is required and how it is obtained.

required.description: Whether approval is mandatory for this action.

required_above.description: Monetary threshold above which approval becomes mandatory. Null means always required (if required is true).

approver_role.description: Organizational role authorized to approve this action.

timeout.description: ISO 8601 duration. How long before approval times out. After timeout, escalation path is followed.

escalation_path.description: Comma-separated organizational roles for escalation if primary approver is unavailable.

credentials.description: Credential brokerage configuration. Defines how downstream tool credentials are injected.

provider.description: Credential provider identifier (e.g., gmail-api, stripe-api, internal-db).

scope.description: Credential scope constraints. Limits what the injected credential can do.

inject_as.description: How the credential is injected into the downstream request.

fail_closed.description: If true, credential injection failure causes action denial. Must be true for production contracts.

output.description: Expected output configuration. Defines the governed object created or modified by this action.

object_type.description: DUAL object type for the output.

initial_state.description: Initial state of the output object after successful execution.

schema_ref.description: Reference to a JSON Schema defining the output object structure.

attestation.description: Evidence level. 'full' records complete execution context on DUAL. 'light' records action metadata only. 'none' skips attestation (development only).

revocable.description: Whether this contract can be revoked. Revocation immediately invalidates all pending approvals.

expiry.description: ISO 8601 datetime. Absolute contract expiration. After this time, no actions can be executed under this contract.

idempotency.description: Idempotency model. 'gateway-enforced' means ATP assigns idempotency keys and prevents duplicate execution. 'tool-native' means the downstream tool handles idempotency. 'unsafe' means retries may cause duplicate side effects.
